_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to leverage implants and/or exploit vulnerabilities on a target system to facilitate intial access.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (prompt injection, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, excessive agency).
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-001 (SP, cosine: 0.460), SP-A-002 (SP, cosine: 0.445).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.314). Shared vocabulary: [vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only terms [ability, access, exploit, facilitate, implants, intial] vs WIDAI-only terms [10, agency, applications, data, excessive, handling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.833 (P=0.838, R=0.827). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Ability to leverage implants and/or exploit vulnerabilities on a target system t" and "Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 f" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.467 > STS 0.314 (gap +0.153). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [vulnerabilities] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 2.6%. Shared tokens: [vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only: [ability, access, exploit, facilitate, implants, intial]. WIDAI-only: [10, agency, applications, data, excessive, handling].
